query.sentence:
Beyond PEEK, which thermoplastic materials can:
- Withstand continuous or intermittent use up to 200 °C
- Offer good chemical compatibility with acid, bases, organic solvents, oils
- Be co extruded with softer polymers such as TPU, TPE, or TPV (e.g., in multilayer or jacketed constructions)
query.application_Y: Withstand 200°C, good chemical compatibility with acid, bases, organic solvents, oils, co extrusion with softer polymers

properties: continuous or intermittent service temperature up to 200 °C
chemical resistance to acids, bases, organic solvents, and oils
high melt strength for co‑extrusion with softer polymers (TPU, TPE, TPV)
constraints: must be compatible with a co‑extruded layer of TPU/TPE/TPV without excessive phase separation
melt temperature must not exceed the softening point of the companion polymer (≈120–140 °C for TPU)
material should not contain fluorinated groups or PFAS chemistry

final_candidate.material_name: Polyphenylene Sulfide (PPS) – Grade: PPS‑R (recycled grade) or PPS‑S (standard)
final_candidate.justification: PPS has a continuous service temperature of 260 °C and can withstand intermittent use at 200 °C. It offers excellent resistance to acids, bases, hydrocarbons, and many organic solvents, making it suitable for aggressive chemical environments. Its melt viscosity (~300–350 Pa·s at 280 °C) is compatible with co‑extrusion when a compatibilizer (e.g., maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene or polyamide) is added to promote interfacial adhesion with TPU/TPE/TPV layers. PPS is non‑PFAS, has good dimensional stability, and can be processed on standard twin‑screw extruders used in laboratory settings.

process_recipe.description: Dry PPS pellets (≤0.5 % moisture) at 120 °C for 2 h to remove residual water.
Dry TPU/TPE/TPV pellets (≤0.5 % moisture) at 80 °C for 1 h.
Load PPS into the main feeder and TPU/TPE/TPV into a secondary feeder of a twin‑screw extruder equipped with a co‑extrusion die.
process_recipe.conditions: Barrel temperature profile: 260–280 °C (zone 1), 240–260 °C (zone 2), 220–240 °C (zone 3); screw speed 50–70 rpm; feed ratio PPS:TPU ≈ 90:10 by weight.

process_recipe.description: Introduce a compatibilizer (e.g., 1–2 wt % maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene) into the PPS feeder to improve interfacial adhesion.
process_recipe.conditions: Same barrel temperature profile as step 3

process_recipe.description: Extrude the co‑extruded strand through a die that forms two layers: outer PPS and inner TPU/TPE/TPV. Cool in a water bath at 20–25 °C.
Pelletize the extruded strand using a pelletizer or cut into desired lengths.
